name: Radio
purpose: Allows the user to select one option from a set of mutually exclusive options.
whenToUse: To select a single option from a list of mutually exclusive choices.
When all available options need to be visible at once for comparison.
whenNotToUse: When multiple options can be selected simultaneously (use Checkbox).
When toggling a single independent setting on or off (use Switch).
When there are many options that would clutter the screen (consider DropdownButton).

accessibility.touchTargets: Ensure minimum touch target of 48x48dp, usually achieved by wrapping the Radio in a RadioListTile or adding padding.
accessibility.semantics: Always associate the radio button with a descriptive label using RadioListTile or Semantics so screen readers can announce its purpose.
accessibility.stateFeedback: Screen readers automatically announce the selected state based on whether value matches the group's selected value.

flutterMappingNotes: Wrap individual Radio or RadioListTile widgets in a RadioGroup ancestor to manage the selected state centrally.
The RadioGroup handles the selected value and onChanged callback, replacing the deprecated pattern of setting groupValue and onChanged on individual Radio widgets.
Assign a unique identifier to the `value` property for each Radio in the group.
Note: `RadioListTile.selected` does not automatically follow the checked state unless explicitly set to match the group's current value.
The radio automatically enters the 'disabled' state when the RadioGroup's `onChanged` is set to null.
Prefer using `RadioListTile` to easily combine a Radio with a Text label and expand the touch target to the full row.
Map circle.selected.border and indicator.selected.background to `activeColor` or use `fillColor` (MaterialStateProperty) for more granular state control.
Use `overlayColor` (MaterialStateProperty) to define visual feedback for pressed, hovered, and focus states.
